defs: Dim or sore with water or rheum; -- said of the eyes.
quotes: His blear eyes ran in gutters to his chin.

defs: Causing or caused by dimness of sight; dim.
quotes: Power to cheat the eye with blear illusion.

records.ety: [OE. bleren; cf. Dan. plire to blink, Sw. plira to twinkle, wink, LG. plieren; perh. from the same root as E. blink. See Blink, and cf. Blur.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Bleared (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Blearing.]

defs: To make somewhat sore or watery, as the eyes; to dim, or blur, as the sight. Figuratively: To obscure (mental or moral perception); to blind; to hoodwink.
quotes: That tickling rheums Should ever tease the lungs and blear the sight.
